agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

This cell is an irrlicht-dispatcher test that happens to be filed under the gemini-cli column. The scenario co-launches a `gemini` process and a non-gemini partner (here claudecode) in the SAME cwd and asserts irrlicht reports two rows under their correct adapter labels with no cross-contamination. The question is purely: does the gemini-cli adapter isolate cleanly from a coexisting adapter? It does, at all three isolation layers irrlicht relies on:

1. **Process matching** — gemini-cli declares its own `Process.Match = CommandPattern{Regex: "bin/gemini"}` (agent.go), disjoint from claudecode's `ExactName{"claude"}`. The scanner runs each adapter's matcher independently and tags every PID with the originating adapter name. gemini-cli additionally drops its heap-bump Node worker via `ExcludeArgv` (isHeapBumpWorker), so it mints exactly one launcher pre-session — no extra ghost to confuse the per-cwd picture.
2. **Transcript roots** — gemini-cli's Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: "~/.gemini/tmp"}` (adapter.go defaultRootDir), disjoint from claudecode's `~/.claude/projects`. The fswatcher attaches a per-root parser-factory from maps.go, so a line under the gemini root can only ever reach the gemini Parser, never claudecode's, and vice versa.
3. **Per-session metrics** — SessionState carries the adapter name; metrics, model, token, and subagent counters all key off the (adapter, session_id) tuple. There is no per-cwd aggregation in the domain, so no path exists for one adapter's tokens/model/parent-links to leak into the other's row.

Spec bullets map cleanly:
- 'Each session appears with its correct adapter label' — adapter is recorded at pre-session creation via the matcher-tagged scan result; gemini-cli binds the launcher PID by cwd (DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine, pid.go).
- 'States transition independently per agent' — the two tailers are different goroutines reading different files; gemini-cli's classifier emits user_message → working and infers turn_done (working→ready) from a final text-only `gemini` message with no open tool calls (parser.go).
- 'No cross-contamination of metrics/parent/subagent counts' — counters are registered per adapter name; gemini-cli's per-message token block and model are only ever applied to its own session.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

irrlicht has no first-class 'workspace' row — the session list is flat and the dashboard groups by cwd only visually. The scenario asks only about per-session correctness, so this is correct, not a gap.

## gemini-cli specifics that matter here

- cwd lives ONLY in the transcript body (`Workspace Directories:` inside the bootstrap `<session_context>`) — gemini-cli is the first adapter with no cwd in the path/process/sidecar. The general `metadata_enricher.EnrichNewSession` cwd fallback to the parser's LastCWD ensures the launcher PID still binds on a short session; without it the transcript session would stay unbound and duplicate against the scanner proc-presession. This is the one provisional edge for a two-arc coexist run: confidence is held at 0.85 until the live recording confirms gemini's session binds cleanly while a second adapter runs in the same cwd. record settles it.
- Live-verified (column onboarding 2026-06-09): a real gemini session binds the launcher PID, transitions working→ready on turn_done, and reports model + cost + tokens. The isolation layers above are unchanged by a coexisting adapter, so the two-distinct-arcs assertion follows.

## Recording

Recorded by scripts/run-cell-multi.sh: it launches the gemini-cli and claudecode interactive drivers concurrently against ONE shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each. This cell stores the gemini-cli transcript for its replay golden; the workspace events.jsonl + expected.jsonl span both adapters and are shared with the claudecode cell (each cell differs only in which transcript it keeps). expected.jsonl is adapter-agnostic (agentA = first to working, agentB = the distinct second), so any two coexisting sessions validate the two-independent-arcs assertion; a third coexisting adapter is tolerated.
caveats: The recipe runs two different agent CLIs concurrently, so the rig (run-cell-multi.sh) needs both gemini and claude installed and authenticated. ONE recording satisfies both matrix rows: the gemini-cli and claudecode cells share the workspace events.jsonl + expected.jsonl, differing only in which transcript each stores for its replay golden.

gemini-cli's cwd is harvested only from the transcript body, so the launcher PID binds via the EnrichNewSession LastCWD fallback. On a coexist run this is the one property not yet live-confirmed (the column was verified solo, not alongside a second adapter); confidence is 0.85 and record promotes it from provisional to settled.
Gemini CLI stops serving Google One / unpaid tiers on 2026-06-18 — record this cell before then on a free tier, or use a paid tier / Antigravity CLI after.

sources.note: gemini-cli declares its own Process.Match = CommandPattern{bin/gemini} and Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.gemini/tmp} — disjoint matcher and transcript root from every other adapter; ExcludeArgv drops the heap-bump worker so only the launcher pre-session is minted.
DiscoverPID binds the launcher (lowest-PID ancestor) by cwd via DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine, narrowed to bin/gemini — independent of any coexisting adapter's processes.

user_message → working; turn_done (working→ready) inferred from a final text-only 'gemini' message with no open tool calls; per-message token/model applied only to this adapter's session — no cross-adapter aggregation path.

geminicli.Agent() is registered in the same adapter list as claudecode/codex/pi; maps.go keys Parsers/PIDDiscoverers/SubagentCounters by Identity.Name, so no shared global parser or counter can leak state between adapters.

sources.ref: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace
sources.note: Three Expected bullets: correct adapter labels, independent transitions, no metrics/parent/subagent cross-contamination.

preconditions: gemini AND claude CLIs on PATH and authenticated (gemini via Google OAuth oauth-personal, set up out-of-band)
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
no other gemini-cli/claudecode session is live in the run cwd
record before 2026-06-18 on a free tier (Gemini CLI free-tier auth deadline)
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches the gemini-cli and claudecode interactive drivers concurrently, both pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD

the isolated daemon observes gemini-cli via its transcript fswatcher (~/.gemini/tmp, binding the launcher PID by cwd harvested from the bootstrap <session_context>) and claudecode via ~/.claude/projects — both keyed off the real $HOME — so it labels each adapter independently
this cell stores the gemini-cli transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alpha

verify: events.jsonl contains TWO distinct session_ids from two different adapters (gemini-cli + claudecode), both keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the two adapters' rows, and gemini-cli's model/tokens stay on its own session
